text.title: Go Channels and Pipeline Patterns Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules every Go Channels and Pipeline Patterns application MUST enforce. HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Only the sender MUST close a channel — never the receiver; never close twice.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Effective Go + Go memory model. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Function signatures MUST type-narrow to `<-chan T` (receive) or `chan<- T` (send) at boundaries.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Go style guide. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Every blocking receive in a long-running goroutine MUST be in a `select` with `<-ctx.Done()`.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Go blog 'pipelines and cancellation'. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Unbuffered is the default; buffered channels require a documented reason (rate-smoothing, drop policy).
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Effective Go. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Consumers MUST `range` over a channel; do not handcraft `for { v, ok := <-ch }` unless needed.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Effective Go. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: PRs MUST pass `go test -race ./...`; merging without is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Go testing docs. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Tests for pipelines MUST use `goleak.VerifyNone(t)` to catch leaks.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: goleak docs. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

section.title: Conclusion targets used by the decision tree

rule.statement: Run the full procedure; emit an artefact passing the output contract.
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when preconditions hold and no duplicate exists.

rule.statement: Skip; surface the missing precondition; do not synthesize.
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when preconditions fail.

rule.statement: Update the existing artefact in place (bump version, refresh last_reviewed).
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when a duplicate is found.
